channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Top Russian officials will hold talks with US in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday
item.description: Top Russian officials will hold talks with U.S. counterparts on restoring ties, negotiating a peaceful settlement to the war in Ukraine and preparing a meeting between Russian President Vladimir Putin and U.S. President Donald Trump, the Kremlin said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels occupy a 2nd major city in Congo's mineral-rich east
item.description: BUKAVU, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels have occupied a second major city in mineral-rich eastern Congo, the government said Sunday, as M23 rebels confirmed they were in the city to restore order after it was abandoned by Congolese forces.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, PASCAL CINAMULA and JANVIER BARHAHIGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia plans to deport 3 violent criminals to Nauru
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia will soon deport three violent criminals including a convicted murderer to Nauru even though they are not citizens of the tiny Pacific atoll, after an Australian court ruled that dangerous immigrants can no longer be imprisoned indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European leaders regroup in Paris for strategy huddle after Trump diplomatic blitz on Ukraine
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has called leaders from key European Union nations and the United Kingdom to his ornate Elysee Palace for an emergency meeting Monday.
item.dc:creator: Raf Casert And Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine's President Zelenskyy travels to United Arab Emirates as momentum grows for war peace talks
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy traveled to the United Arab Emirates late Sunday as momentum grows for potential peace talks ending Moscow’s war on the country.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Woman killed as Israeli forces fire on returnees to southern Lebanon ahead of ceasefire deadline
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A woman was killed and several other people wounded Sunday when Israeli forces opened fire on a group of residents attempting to return to the village of Houla in southern Lebanon, the Lebanese state-run news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Putin 'wants to stop fighting' in Ukraine
item.description: President Trump said Russian President Vladimir Putin “wants to stop fighting” and shot down a suggestion that Putin’s ambitions could extend beyond Ukraine’s borders. “I think he wants to stop fighting. I see that. We spoke long and hard.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Rubio spars with Brennan over Vance’s Munich speech
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio sparred with CBS host Margaret Brennan over Vice President Vance’s speech in Munich.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: American Sagui Dekel Chen is home after being released by Hamas: Rubio
item.description: American Sagui Dekel Chen is “home and safe” with his family, one day after he was released by Hamas, Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed Sunday. Chen was among the three men released in Khan Younis on Saturday in the latest victory for the fragile ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats split over how to combat Trump's USAID shutdown
item.description: Senate Democrats are split over how to push back against President Trump’s attempts to slash the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), an embattled front line in the larger fight against the administration’s effort to overhaul the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Mullin says to 'trust the process' in Trump’s Russia-Ukraine peace talks
item.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-Okla.) says he thinks people need to “trust the process” in President Trump’s peace talks with Russian and Ukrainian leaders. “Right now, people are dying every single day.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Crenshaw says Europeans 'should be threatening things to' Putin that cause them discomfort
item.description: Rep. Dan Crenshaw (R-Texas) said Europeans "should be threatening things to" Russian President Vladimir Putin that cause them discomfort in an interview that aired Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: US citizen allegedly carrying cannabis detained in Moscow, charged with narcotics smuggling: Russian media
item.description: An American was detained in Russia for allegedly bringing cannabis-laced gummies through customs at a Moscow airport, Russian media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants name 3 Israeli men to be freed in next hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group on Friday welcomed the “joyous news” that three men, all taken from the same kibbutz on Oct. 7, 2023, are expected to be freed on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners held in Israeli jails.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas to free another American in Saturday hostage release
item.description: Hamas has released the names of the next three hostages who will be released on Saturday Feb. 15, including American-Israeli Sagui Dekel-Chen, the second American to be released since Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: King of Jordan urges patience as Trump presses Gaza takeover
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday dug in on his vision for a U.S. takeover of the Gaza Strip as he sat alongside King Abdullah II of Jordan, who expressed reservations about the idea.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says ceasefire deal should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all hostages by Saturday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that a precarious ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all the remaining hostages it is holding in Gaza by midday on Saturday — though he also said that such a decision would be up to Israel.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and SAM MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
